title: Incorrect terminology in reference to a much hated theory
display_title: Incorrect terminology in reference to a much hated theory
wikitext:
[]: Incorrect terminology in reference to a much hated theory             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:01 pm The idea of a “Gainax Multiverse” is rarely mentioned, but is attacked when it is. However, this theory is actually one of a Gainax Universe, not Multiverse. In fact, the True Gainax Multiverse is occasionally hinted at by Gainax. But what is an actual Gainax Multiverse? The shows exist in separate Universes, but the same Multiverse. And there are a couple of people who are Sliders, people that can hop Universes. One of these people is Kaworu. Evidence: (Note that I haven't played Super Robot Wars or seen Rebuild, so anything from there may be incorrect.) ·In Super Robot Wars, Kaworu mentions events from other continuities. ·In Rebuild, Kaworu says the infamous line about seeing Shinji again. ·Kaworu's personality hardly changes between continuities, unlike Shinji or Rei. And if this is true, it brings a very satisfying answer to the concurrency debate: All theories are true, but in Alternate Universes. Hell, maybe Rei is also a Slider, and a really good actor. Also, for the Rebuild as a sequel theory, it is a sequel, but only from the perspective of Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:07 pm The final lines of 2.0 are the only real hint at the continuities being connected, but it's still one that carried a lot of ambiguity that it can easily be taken out of context. Not really proof. Besides, the original series did end happily, somewhat. Kaworu does not have to make Shinji happy this time around because he ended happily last time. It's was very negative in a dark world, but Shinji and Asuka finally know how each other feels and all the 'dead' souls can come back if they wish. The Tokyo-3 area may be a wasteland now, but Instrumentality may have left half the world in good condition. The AT-field bomb from the MP Evas was not global in scale.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:12 pm No. Please, god, no. Please tell me we're not going to let sequel theory threads leak into NGE discussion. Don't do this. That terrible, hissing sound you hear is the last remnants of artistic integrity being methodically sapped from the original work.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:12 pm That "seeing again" thingy is just a translation bug/anomaly, afaik. It´s only a LOOSELY implied interpretation and Kaworu never said words like that LITERALLY in Japanese. But I do support the whole "Rebuild is a sequel of sorts" idea though.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:12 pm I haven't read any of the other continuities. So I wouldn't know. Sound interesting, though. Though, it seem similar to the inconsistencies in any other fictional universe that had different writers on it. Heck, The Joker's origin story keeps changing in almost every Batman comic book. Sometimes he's even depicted as being a sane person pretending to be insane. Other times he's really just insane. And even the Joker references these inconsistencies in The Killing Joke , saying he likes to pick which ever history suits him best for whatever agenda he had. And, just like in Batman, I don't know if any of this has anything to do with the actual story/universe/multiverse/whatever of Eva's multiple stories. (Except for maybe Rebuild, seeing as how that's Anno's work.) It's just the result of multiple writers writing stuff. As for Kaworu's line, I'm waiting until the pros translate that one out.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:31 pm oh wow. no. I suppose episode 7 of TTGL is your other evidence?

[]:             Quote Post by Zoop » Tue Aug 10, 2010 3:31 pm I don't find these to be strong arguements. And when put out of context, it kinda sounds like a lame excuse to justify inconsistencies. Let's face it, the very power of Evangelion, is it's secrecy, it's unanswered questions, it's effect it can have on your emotions. It's a status quo they (Gainax/Anno) will try to maintain, as it's one hell of a milkcow for merchandise. People want to get paid for their work, y'know. There will never be a conclusive explanation to everything in Evangelion. Also, keep in account that people DO make mistakes, or simply don't do everything on purpose. So be careful not to fabricate an answer for things that weren't meant to be explained. I'd say Rebuild is either a direct sequel (only time will tell) or an alternate universe. As for the other works I'd say the Joker-syndrome (and many other DC/Marvell/millions of other examples) FreakyFilmFan4ever mentions applies very well. There isn't a universal law that applies to everything in the Evangelion multiverse, don't forget, it's fiction, they can change things as they please. If you seek answers for your questions, I'd say: stick to the direct canon. I can see why this theory get's fired down easily

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 11, 2010 1:12 am slothen wrote: oh wow. no. I suppose episode 7 of TTGL is your other evidence?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 11, 2010 11:29 am Yea, getting popped like a pez dispenser always leads to interdimensional traversing powers. Rebuild is too ambiguous, and Super Robot Wars makes a point of deliberately altering continuities so they can all fit together. You might as well argue that Ideon created all mecha universes.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 11, 2010 11:40 am AuraTwilight wrote: Rebuild is too ambiguous

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Aug 11, 2010 12:59 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, getting popped like a pez dispenser always leads to interdimensional traversing powers. Super Robot Wars makes a point of deliberately altering continuities so they can all fit together.

[]:             Quote Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Wed Aug 11, 2010 8:59 pm I would like to point some things out about Eva and SRW. Kaworu's status as a Time Lord dimension-travelling entity is not unique in the SRW multiverse, and the SRW multiverse is self-contained. Gilliam Jaeger has travelled through multiple SRW universes within that multiverse, noticed that they are interconnected, and also realized that they are inescapable. That entire multiverse is an artificial connection of worlds brought about by Banpresto. Because of this the Kaworu seen in SRW is remembering things from other SRW universes and not the original, Eva universe . If that's difficult to get your head around, here's a diagram: Again, the SRW Kaworu is not the same as Eva Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 11, 2010 9:15 pm Hoshikawa-kun wrote: here's a diagram:

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 12, 2010 1:34 pm I was thinking that the manga was Kaworu's first Universe.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Aug 12, 2010 2:03 pm Call me a snob, but i can't believe people are seriously considering Super Robot Wars in an Evangelion related discussion as.... anything, really. Come on, it's not even a strictly Evangelion related product. It's not even a Gainax product.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Thu Aug 12, 2010 3:47 pm I jokingly suggested a similar "Kaworu is a slider" theory when the first Rebuild film came out and we had our first sequel-or-not debates. I wasn't suggesting Kaworu could cross over between the two universes - Hoshikawa-kun's chart nails this situation - but rather that Anno might have used SRW's Kaworu as an influence for the Eva Universe's Kaworu. Who knows, it might even have been Anno's original idea that the SRW Kaworu is a slider, since he apparently had some creative input on the storylines of the relevant games. This idea of course implies that Anno is such a shamless hack/troll/money-grubber (pick your favorite) that he would recycle a plot twist from a videogame series notorious of its clusterfuckery. Therefore, I'd like to emphasize that my "theory" was just a random quip. There's no real evidence for it as far as I can tell, and no-one should endorse it seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 13, 2010 2:11 pm I'd like to suggest that everyone voiced by Akira Ishida, including the voice actor himself, is Kaworu pulling Slider shenanigans.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 29, 2010 6:48 pm Is it safe to admit that this was a troll thread?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Mon Aug 30, 2010 4:53 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: That "seeing again" thingy is just a translation bug/anomaly, afaik. It´s only a LOOSELY implied interpretation and Kaworu never said words like that LITERALLY in Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 30, 2010 1:40 pm Yea, that line is old news, and it doesn't have to mean anything that you're thinking it means. SPOILER: Show
